description_long: This is a small, simple cabin with a rectangular footprint and a dark brown gable roof. The walls are light-colored, likely made of wood or a similar material, and the structure sits isolated on a flat, circular ground plane. The roof is steeply pitched, with no visible overhangs or dormers, and the cabin lacks any visible ornamentation or architectural detailing. There are no visible windows or doors on any side, giving the structure a very minimal and utilitarian appearance. The overall design suggests a basic shelter, possibly for temporary or seasonal use, with a focus on function over aesthetics. The cabin's compact size and plain exterior make it suitable for rural or natural settings, though it could also appear in urban parks or as a small outbuilding.
description: A small, plain cabin with a dark gable roof and light walls, lacking visible windows or doors. Its simple, utilitarian design suggests a basic shelter.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from above with the gable roof clearly visible and no windows or doors apparent.
description_view_1: This side view shows the long side of the cabin with the roof ridge running parallel, still no openings visible.
description_view_2: The opposite end of the cabin is visible, again showing the gable roof and plain walls without features.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, with the long wall and roof ridge in clear view, no architectural details present.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a steep pitch, no overhangs or dormers, covered in dark material
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin
attribute_door_placement: No exterior doors are visible on any side of the cabin
